analysis_meta.transcript_language: no audio — transcript file contains '(no audio)' only
analysis_meta.notes: Recording has NO audio track — transcript is empty. Analysis is based entirely on visual frames. Product is demoed over a Google Meet call (room ede-hxjp-uod). Presenter is Gari Zeldich sharing screen. The trackbox.ai splash screen (frame_000717.jpg) confirms the product name includes the '.ai' suffix. Overview.json mis-labeled frame_000148.jpg as 'Lead detail modal — Margarett Coby' when it actually shows the Campaigns list; other overview frame descriptions are accurate.

product_identification.product_url: trackbox.ai (confirmed from splash screen logo in frame_000717.jpg)
product_identification.product_category: Affiliate marketing CRM / performance marketing lead management platform
product_identification.positioning: A full-stack affiliate and lead management system for performance marketing networks: tracks leads from acquisition through FTD conversion, manages affiliate relationships, routes leads to brands, detects fraud, and provides granular analytics.
product_identification.target_market: Performance marketing networks, affiliate managers, and media buyers operating CPA/lead-gen campaigns — particularly in high-regulation verticals (finance, forex, crypto) based on campaign names like 'Campaign for offer FX ETH/XRP/Bitcoin'.
prospect_pain_points: Need to monitor affiliate performance in real time across multiple brands and campaigns
Need to detect and filter fraudulent leads (bots, duplicates, mismatched phone/country) before passing them to brands
Need to track FTD (First Time Deposit) conversions and reconcile affiliate payouts
Need visibility into which leads were accepted, rejected, or timed out by brand CRMs
Need to route leads automatically based on rules (timing, caps, destination brand)
Need to manage parameter replacement (UTM/tracking params) per affiliate and campaign
Need security oversight: employees without 2FA, logins from new locations, unauthorized exports
Need to assign lead quality scores and distinguish Q-Leads from raw total leads
Need per-affiliate performance analytics with customizable metrics
Need to bulk-import and inject lead lists into specific campaigns and affiliates

Dashboard.description: Executive overview page displaying aggregated KPI metrics, affiliate performance summary, live lead feeds, a multi-series trend chart, traffic map, and security alerts for the selected date range.

Traffic KPI Cards.description: Shows top-of-funnel traffic metrics with period-over-period comparison.
Traffic KPI Cards.notes: Percentage change vs prior period shown in green (positive) or red (negative) below each card.

Finance KPI Cards.description: Financial performance metrics including revenue, payout to affiliates, and net profit.
Finance KPI Cards.notes: Net Profit displayed in red when negative, highlighting financial loss on the selected period. Demo data shows significant negative margin.

Conversion KPI Cards.description: Lead quality and conversion funnel metrics.
Conversion KPI Cards.notes: Q-Leads and Q-FTDs are 'qualified' lead counts, distinct from raw totals.

Security Alert Banner.description: Inline security warning on the dashboard prompting action on compliance issues.
ui_elements: Some employees don't have 2FA enabled. Ask them to activate it.
Security Alert Banner.notes: Links directly to the Security section for remediation.

Monthly Goals.description: Progress bars showing leads and FTD deposit goals vs actuals.
Monthly Goals.notes: Configurable target numbers set in System Settings.

Online Users & Pending Affiliates.description: Live counters for currently active users and affiliates awaiting approval.

Top Affiliates Table.description: Ranked leaderboard table of top-performing affiliates for the selected period.
Top Affiliates Table.notes: Rows include: Affiliate3 (54.55% CR), AffiliateBloc (various), Affiliate1, Affiliate2. Up to 11 affiliates shown; count configurable.

TCC / Expert Live Feed.description: Two live feed panels showing the most recent incoming leads with country of origin.
TCC / Expert Live Feed.notes: TCC and Expert appear to be two distinct brand destinations receiving leads in real time. Country flags indicate lead origin geography.

Statistics Chart.description: Multi-line trend chart overlaying key metrics over the date range.
Statistics Chart.notes: Legend items are togglable. Shows the last ~2 weeks of data.

Traffic Map.description: Geographic heatmap/visualization of traffic by country.
Traffic Map.notes: Metric selector allows switching between Impressions, Clicks, etc.

Date Range / Quick Tabs.description: Date filter at top with preset quick-select tabs.
Date Range / Quick Tabs.notes: All sections share this top-level filter. 'Get Data' must be manually clicked after changing the range.

Leads.description: Central lead management module. Lists all incoming leads across affiliates and campaigns, supports detailed inspection of individual leads, fraud analysis, bulk operations, and list import.

Lead List Table.description: Paginated data table of all leads with sortable/filterable columns.
Lead List Table.notes: Multiple saved view tabs allow different column/filter presets. Views can be named. Columns are fully customizable via Customize Columns modal.

Filter Panel.description: Slide-out filter panel for narrowing the lead list.
Filter Panel.notes: Filter panel is collapsible. After applying filters the table reloads.

Customize Columns Modal.description: Modal for toggling which columns are visible in the leads table.
Customize Columns Modal.notes: Bot column shows automated traffic detection indicators in red/orange tags when enabled.

Lead Detail Modal.description: Full-detail overlay for a single lead record showing all tracked data, API integration logs, and analytical tabs.
ui_elements: Lead name (e.g. Margarett Coby, Elida Serna, Dion Wiers)
Lead Detail Modal.notes: The API Request/Response sub-tabs show the raw JSON payload sent to the brand CRM and the brand's response. 'Result Seems' likely indicates a verdict label on the push result. Empty state shows 'No Data' when no API log is available.

Fraud Detection Tab.description: Per-lead fraud risk scoring and integration with a fraud API.
Fraud Detection Tab.notes: Available Credits 999 suggests a credit-based fraud API (likely third-party). The fraud score is computed per lead.

Bulk Actions (Bottom Action Bar).description: Multi-select checkboxes on lead rows reveal a bottom action bar for batch operations.
Bulk Actions (Bottom Action Bar).notes: Inject Leads opens the 'Where to inject?' modal. Fraud Check presumably triggers the fraud API for selected leads in batch.

Where to Inject? (Import Modal).description: Modal for routing selected leads to a specific campaign and affiliate.
Where to Inject? (Import Modal).notes: Allows rerouting leads across affiliates/campaigns in bulk. Autologin option suggests generating auto-login links for the leads.

Create New Rule (Upload List).description: Modal for importing a lead list from a file or from an existing list source.
ui_elements: Drag and drop files here to upload
Create New Rule (Upload List).notes: Download Template allows downloading a correctly formatted CSV/XLSX for upload. This appears to be a lead list import feature tied to distribution rules.

Campaigns.description: Manages the campaign catalog — the bridge between affiliate traffic and brand destinations. Each campaign belongs to a brand and has privacy and status controls.

Campaign List Table.description: Paginated table of all campaigns with status and visibility management.
Campaign List Table.notes: 2485 total campaigns indicate a large-scale operation. Privacy (Public/Private) controls which affiliates can see and use a campaign. Brands include: Mock, AfxCenters, Shabbai, Antiblock, Mock5, Mock4. Campaign naming convention e.g. 'Campaign for offer FX ETH' indicates vertical-specific campaigns.

Statistics.description: Performance analytics module showing aggregated metrics per affiliate (and optionally per campaign, brand, country) for the selected date range.

Performance Graph Table.description: Main statistics table with one row per affiliate/campaign entity showing all key metrics.
Performance Graph Table.notes: Totals row at bottom: 288 Impressions, 258 Clicks, 111 Q-Leads, 19 Q-FTDs, €6,312.66 payout, €6,300 revenue. ROI is shown as negative for some affiliates (e.g. -87.07%, -83.4%). 'Hard Failures' and 'Unassigned' appear to track API push failures and unrouted leads.

Filter Panel.description: Filters for segmenting statistics by affiliate, country, brand.
Filter Panel.notes: Date range options for the filter panel are separate from the top-level date filter.

Customize Columns Modal.description: Drag-and-drop column customization for the statistics table.
Customize Columns Modal.notes: Two-panel design: Available Fields (left) and In Use (right) with add/remove buttons.

Insights Modal.description: Per-affiliate drill-down modal with performance charts and activity analysis.
Insights Modal.notes: Accessible by clicking a row in the statistics table. Performance tab shows trend lines over time. Some affiliates show 'No Data' if they have no activity in the period. Hourly Activity and Auto Login tabs also available.

Security.description: Security and compliance monitoring module tracking 2FA adoption, suspicious logins, data exports, and password resets.

Security Alert Cards.description: Three severity-coded alert cards summarizing the current security posture.
ui_elements: Your system at risk: activate 2FA (red banner)
Security Alert Cards.notes: 30 employees lacking 2FA is flagged as high risk. The 'Show Low severity risks' toggle suggests a risk tiering system.

Password Resets Table.description: Detailed list of recent password reset events with user and role information.
Password Resets Table.notes: Roles visible: Affiliate, MasterAffiliate. 'System' as the 'Changed By' actor indicates automated or admin-initiated resets.

Falcon.description: FTD (First Time Deposit) management module. Tracks conversion events reported by brand CRMs, allows manual FTD updates, and manages payout reconciliation.

FTD Records Table.description: Full list of FTD events with affiliate, campaign, status, and payout data.
FTD Records Table.notes: Most FTDs show 'Approved' status; some show 'Declined'. CPA in the Actions column indicates cost-per-acquisition model. Payout and Revenue columns track financial settlements. 'FTD Update' (red) suggests a manual upload or correction flow. Country flags shown per record (ES=Spain, GB=UK).

Replace Params.description: Parameter replacement rule engine that substitutes tracking parameters (UTM, affiliate IDs, custom params) when leads are pushed to brands — enabling flexible parameter mapping per affiliate/country/campaign combination.

Replace Params Table.description: Table of active parameter replacement rules.
Replace Params Table.notes: Rules include 'test' (All Affiliates, BRAND TEST, Created 13/02/26) and 'Test Aff Trafalgan' (Aiest, +243 country code, All Campaigns). 'Save in Leads' toggle controls whether the replaced params are written back to the lead record.

Distribution.description: Lead routing and distribution rule engine. Defines where leads are sent (brand/CRM destinations), with timing controls, daily/hourly caps, and per-rule status management.

Distribution Summary Counters.description: Top-of-page aggregate limit counters showing current usage vs configured maximum.
Distribution Summary Counters.notes: Limits appear to be soft caps shown for awareness. Rules Limit (2/10) caps the number of active distribution rules.

Distribution Rules Table.description: Paginated list of lead routing rules with full destination and timing details.
Distribution Rules Table.notes: Destination format: 'Affiliate: [name] Campaign: [name]'. Timing 'DB_TO_LIST — Now' suggests database-to-list immediate push mode. Status values: Done, Running. Cap column controls max leads per rule.

System Settings.description: Global configuration panel for platform branding, lead processing behavior, fraud detection, registration forms, and integrations.

General Tab.description: Core platform preferences and operational defaults.
ui_elements: Theme Color (color palette picker with 8 preset swatches)
Include Free parameters for injected leads (toggle)
General Tab.notes: Theme Color supports full color customization. White-label capability implied by System Title and Brand Logo controls.

Right Panel — Defaults & Validation.description: Default behaviors for invalid transactions, duplicate blocking, and lead prioritization.
ui_elements: Block leads if IP and phone country mismatch (toggle)
Replace Params Rule Priority (dropdown: First Rule)
Right Panel — Defaults & Validation.notes: Country mismatch blocking is a fraud prevention control. 'Replace Params Rule Priority: First Rule' means when multiple rules match, the first matching rule wins.

Employees.description: User management module for internal staff and affiliate accounts.

Employee List Table.description: Full list of system users with role, status, and activity metadata.
Employee List Table.notes: Role 'GeneralManager' visible in demo data. 'JTA' column purpose not clear from visual alone — likely a boolean flag or date. 'Last Promoted Sheet' may track affiliate tier promotions.

Left Sidebar Navigation.description: Icon-only collapsible left sidebar providing access to all product modules.

Navigation Items.description: All navigation destinations visible in the expanded sidebar.
ui_elements: Replace Params (visible in Replace Params frames)
Navigation Items.notes: Navigation uses icon-only display by default; text labels appear on hover or when expanded. At least 15 distinct modules are accessible.

ux_ui.design_style: enterprise dark-mode dashboard — dense data, low visual noise, consistent dark navy/charcoal backgrounds with orange and blue accent colors
ux_ui.navigation_pattern: narrow left sidebar (icon-only, collapsible) + top date filter bar with quick-select tabs + main content area; module pages use sub-tabs for view variants
ux_ui.color_scheme: Primary background: #1a1f2e (dark navy/charcoal); Accent 1: #FF6B00 orange (primary action buttons: New Lead, Get Data, Create); Accent 2: #3B82F6 blue (secondary actions, highlights); Success/positive: green; Error/negative: red; Status badges: green (Active/Approved), red (Declined/risk), orange (warnings)
ux_ui.framework_hints: Custom-built UI — consistent component library with dark-themed cards, skeleton loading states, and modal dialogs. Likely React-based given the SPA navigation patterns. Chat bubble widget visible in bottom-right (third-party support widget).

key_screens.description: Full-screen dark loading screen with the trackbox.ai logo centered — white 'b' monogram with 'trackbox.ai' text and a glowing purple/blue circular animation ring.

The main dashboard showing all KPI cards (Traffic, Finance, Conversion, Security), monthly goal progress bars, Online Users, Top 11 Affiliates table, TCC/Expert live feeds, Statistics multi-line chart, and Traffic Map.
notable_elements: TCC/Expert live feeds with country flags

key_screens.description: Security page showing three risk alert cards: 30 employees without 2FA (red/high severity), 14 logins from new locations (medium), 0 exports in 7 days, 4 password resets (medium).

key_screens.screen_name: Leads — List with Bulk Action Bar
key_screens.description: Leads table with multiple rows selected showing all columns and the bottom bulk-action bar with Fraud Check, Fix Customer Id Param, Notes, Change Sale Status, Change Type, Inject Leads, Delete, Cancel. The 'Where to inject?' popup is open.
notable_elements: Campaign, Box, Brand, Country, Email, Phone, Type columns

key_screens.description: Campaigns page listing 2485 total campaigns. Columns: ID, Name, Brand, Brand ID, Status, Privacy, Note. Campaign names include crypto/forex offers. Create Campaign button visible.
notable_elements: Brands: Mock, AfxCenters, Shabbai, Antiblock, Mock5, Mock4

key_screens.description: Statistics page showing the Performance Graph table with all affiliates listed and their metrics: Impressions, Clicks, Q-Leads, CTL, Q-FTDs, CR, Total Payout, Revenue, ROI, Late FTDs, Hard Failures, Unassigned, Injected.
notable_elements: Total row: 288 Impressions, 111 Q-Leads, 19 Q-FTDs, €6,312.66 payout

key_screens.description: Replace Params page with 3 parameter replacement rules shown. Columns: ID, Name, Affiliate, Country, Campaign, Status, Save in Leads, Updated, Note. Create New Params button.
notable_elements: ON/OFF toggles for Status and Save in Leads

key_screens.description: Distribution page showing daily/hourly/rules limit counters at top and 80 distribution rules in a table with destination affiliate/campaign pairs, timing, and status.

System Settings General tab showing white-label options (System Title, Brand Logo, Theme Color with 8 color swatches, Light/Dark toggle), operational settings, and right panel with duplicate blocking and lead validation controls.
notable_elements: Theme Color picker with 8 swatches + custom
Block leads if IP and phone country mismatch
Replace Params Rule Priority: First Rule

key_screens.description: Falcon module showing FTD records with detailed columns including Found Date, Customer Id, Email, Affiliate, Campaign, Country, Status (Approved/Declined), CPA action, Conversion Date, Payout, Revenue.

Customize Columns modal with two-panel drag interface showing Available Fields (Insights, ID, TCC Actions, Installs, CTR, Uninstalls, Total Leads, Total FTDs) and In Use (Impressions, Clicks, Q-Leads, CTL, Q-FTDs, CR, Total Payout, Revenue).

steps: Step 1: Open Dashboard, select 'Last 7 Days' quick tab, click 'Get Data'
Step 2: Review KPI cards for Traffic (Impressions, Clicks, CTL), Finance (Revenue, Payout, Net Profit), Conversion (Q-Leads, Q-FTDs, CR)
Step 3: Scan Top Affiliates table for performance ranking
Step 4: Review TCC/Expert live feeds for recent lead country distribution
Step 5: Check Security alert banner; if 2FA risk flagged, click 'View Security Report'
Step 6: Navigate to Statistics for per-affiliate breakdown with full metrics table
user_scenarios.outcome: Manager has full overview of network health, financial performance, and compliance posture for the period.

steps: Step 1: Navigate to Leads section
Step 2: Apply filters (Affiliate, Country, Brand) or search by email to find suspect leads
Step 3: Click a lead row to open the Lead Detail modal
Step 4: Review Details tab for contact info, tracking parameters, and lead source
Step 5: Open Fraud Detection tab to see Fraud Score and available fraud API credits
Step 6: Check Auto Logs by Body for the API Request sent to the brand CRM and the API Response received
Step 7: Review 'Result Seems' verdict on the push result
Step 8: Enable Bot column via Customize Columns to see bot detection flags across the lead list
user_scenarios.outcome: Analyst identifies fraudulent or low-quality leads, understands why specific leads were rejected by brands, and can take bulk action (Fraud Check, Delete, Change Status).

steps: Step 1: Navigate to Leads section
Step 2: Click 'New Rule' / 'Create New Rule' button
Step 3: Enter Rule Name in the Create New Rule modal
Step 4: Select source: 'Upload A New List' and drag/drop the CSV file onto the upload area (or select 'Loading List' for existing)
Step 5: Click 'Create New Rule' to trigger the import
Step 6: Navigate to Distribution to set up routing rules for the new leads
Step 7: Alternatively, select leads from the list table and use 'Inject Leads' bulk action to route them to a specific Campaign + Affiliate via the 'Where to inject?' modal
user_scenarios.outcome: Imported lead list is processed, assigned to the appropriate campaign and affiliate, and pushed to the brand CRM.

steps: Step 1: Navigate to Statistics
Step 2: Select date range, optionally open filter panel and filter by specific Brand or Country
Step 3: Optionally customize columns via Customize Columns modal to show ROI, Late FTDs, Hard Failures
Step 4: Scan the Performance Graph table for affiliates with poor ROI or high Hard Failures
Step 5: Click on an affiliate row to open the Insights modal
Step 6: Review Performance tab trend lines (Impressions, Q-Leads, CTL, Q-FTDs) over time
Step 7: Switch to Hourly Activity tab to identify peak traffic hours
Step 8: Switch to Auto Login tab for session data
user_scenarios.outcome: Manager identifies underperforming affiliates, understands traffic patterns, and can negotiate or adjust campaign parameters accordingly.

steps: Step 1: Navigate to Security section
Step 2: Review alert cards: identify employees without 2FA (30 in demo), logins from new locations (14), and password resets (4)
Step 3: Enable 'Show Low severity risks' to see all issues
Step 4: Review the password reset detail table to identify which accounts were reset and by whom
Step 5: Contact employees without 2FA to enforce activation
Step 6: Investigate suspicious login locations
user_scenarios.outcome: Admin has a complete picture of security risks and can take remediation steps for compliance.

technology_stack.frontend: React (inferred from SPA navigation patterns, component-based architecture, skeleton loading states)
integrations: Brand CRM APIs (lead push via HTTP with JSON request/response, visible in lead detail API logs)
Third-party Fraud Detection API (credit-based, 999 credits visible — likely IPQS, Ekata, or similar)
Google Meet (used for the demo call)
Third-party support chat widget (floating chat bubble in bottom-right of all pages)
technology_stack.api_type: REST (JSON request/response payloads visible in Lead Detail API logs)

recording_analysis.presenter_role: Gari Zeldich — likely a sales representative or product specialist at trackbox.ai
recording_analysis.audience_role: adi koren and Петр Белов — likely prospects or existing clients evaluating the platform's capabilities
recording_analysis.structure_summary: The recording begins with a Google Meet pre-demo setup (file dialog, 2 minutes of call participants without screen share). Screen share starts at ~117s with the Dashboard. The demo then walks through: Dashboard overview → Security → back to Dashboard (reloading) → Leads (extensive browsing, ~15 min) → Campaigns (briefly shown) → Statistics → Replace Params → Distribution → System Settings → Falcon → back to Leads → Statistics again (with Insights drill-downs) → Leads (filter panel, customize columns, bot detection) → Employees → platform reload (splash screen) → Dashboard again → meeting end screen. Leads and Statistics receive by far the most demo time.
key_selling_points: Complete affiliate performance visibility: Impressions → Clicks → Leads → Q-Leads → FTDs → Revenue in one dashboard
Fraud detection at the lead level with per-lead Fraud Score and bulk Fraud Check
Bot detection column in the leads table showing automated traffic
Full API integration transparency — see the exact JSON pushed to brand CRMs and their responses
Flexible lead routing via Distribution rules with daily/hourly/rule caps
Parameter replacement engine for customizing tracking params per affiliate/country/campaign
Per-affiliate Insights modal with hourly activity analysis
White-label capability: custom System Title, Brand Logo, Theme Color
Security module with 2FA compliance tracking and suspicious login monitoring
Multiple saved view tabs on the Leads page for different team member workflows
Bulk lead operations: inject, fraud check, status change, delete
acknowledged_weaknesses: Net Profit is negative in the demo data (-4,212.66€ on 2,100€ revenue) — not addressed during demo, may raise questions
Statistics Insights modal shows 'No Data' for several affiliates, suggesting incomplete data coverage
Rules Limit capped at 10 (showing 2/10) — may be a plan-based constraint that limits scalability
Distribution shows only 80 rules; combined with the 10-rule limit indicator this may indicate plan-based restrictions
Loading states are frequent and visible throughout the demo, suggesting the platform can be slow to fetch data
audience_questions_and_reactions: No audio — cannot determine audience reactions or questions
objection_handling: No audio — cannot determine objection handling

functions.purpose: Capture incoming leads from affiliate traffic, assign them to campaigns and brands, and track every step of their lifecycle from click to deposit.
data_flow: Step 1: Lead is generated by affiliate traffic (impression → click → registration form fill)
Step 2: System receives lead data via API or CSV upload, stores with affiliate/campaign/brand attribution
Step 3: Lead appears in Leads table with all metadata: Email, Country, Phone, Type, Status, tracking params
user_steps: Navigate to Leads section
Use filters (Affiliate, Country, Brand, Date) to segment the lead pool
Click any lead to open the detail modal and inspect all fields
configuration_options: Multiple saved view tabs with different column sets
functions.automation_level: semi-automatic — lead data is captured automatically; routing and action require user configuration
functions.ui_complexity: moderate — table is dense with many columns, but column customization and saved views help manage cognitive load
edge_cases_handled: 'No Data' empty state when a lead has no API push record
Bot detection flags in dedicated column
functions.verdict: Well-implemented core module. The Lead Detail modal is particularly powerful with the API Request/Response log giving full push transparency. The main friction point is the manual 'Get Data' click required after every filter change.

functions.purpose: Identify fraudulent, low-quality, or bot-generated leads before they cost the network money through invalid payouts to affiliates.
data_flow: Step 1: Lead is received and optionally submitted to a third-party fraud API (credit-based)
Step 2: Fraud API returns a score and verdict stored on the lead record
Step 3: Score visible in Fraud Score column in the table and detailed in the Fraud Detection tab of the Lead Detail modal
user_steps: Enable 'Fraud Score' column via Customize Columns
Open a lead's detail modal and switch to Fraud Detection tab
Or select multiple leads and use 'Fraud Check' bulk action
configuration_options: Fraud Detection tab in System Settings (configuration not shown)
Available Credits (999 in demo) limit total checks
Bot column in Leads table for bot detection
functions.automation_level: semi-automatic — fraud scoring requires user-initiated check or automatic trigger (not confirmed from visual alone)
functions.ui_complexity: simple to view, moderate to act on
edge_cases_handled: Credit system prevents unlimited automated checks
Separate Bot detection column for client-side bot identification
functions.verdict: Solid feature set. The credit-based model is standard for fraud API integrations. Combining Fraud Score with Bot detection and country/IP mismatch blocking (in System Settings) gives multiple layers of protection. Missing: no visible automatic flagging or threshold-based auto-rejection of high-fraud-score leads.

functions.purpose: Automatically route leads to the correct brand CRM endpoint based on configurable rules with timing, caps, and destination mapping.
data_flow: Step 1: User configures distribution rules in the Distribution module (Affiliate + Campaign destination, timing mode, caps)
Step 2: System evaluates incoming leads against active rules and routes them to matching destinations
Step 3: Push result (API Request/Response) is logged on each lead record; successful leads counted in 'Successful Leads' column
user_steps: Navigate to Distribution
Click 'Create New Rule'
Define rule: name, destination (Affiliate + Campaign), timing (DB_TO_LIST — Now or scheduled), daily/hourly caps
Activate rule — status shows as Running or Done
functions.automation_level: fully automatic once rules are configured
functions.ui_complexity: moderate — the rules table is clear but the rule creation flow was not fully shown
edge_cases_handled: Rules Limit (2/10) prevents unbounded rule creation
Done vs Running status tracks rule lifecycle
functions.verdict: Core routing functionality appears solid. The 10-rule limit is a notable constraint for large networks with many brands/affiliates. The daily/hourly caps are essential for brands that don't want to be overwhelmed. Missing: no visible A/B split routing, priority weighting between rules, or fallback rule configuration.

functions.purpose: Give affiliate managers a comprehensive, filterable view of traffic and conversion performance per affiliate, with drill-down charts and customizable metrics.
data_flow: Step 1: User selects date range and optional filters (Affiliate, Country, Brand) then clicks 'Get Data'
Step 2: System aggregates all lead, click, and FTD data for the period into per-row metrics
Step 3: Table displays aggregated metrics; user can click a row to open the Insights modal with trend charts
user_steps: Navigate to Statistics
Select date range (preset or custom)
Optionally apply filters via Filter panel
Optionally customize columns
Click an affiliate row to open Insights modal
Switch between Performance, Hourly Activity, Auto Login tabs in the modal
configuration_options: MyView tab for saved personal view
functions.automation_level: fully manual — user must click 'Get Data' to refresh
functions.ui_complexity: moderate — many columns can be overwhelming; column customization and MyView help
edge_cases_handled: 'No Data' empty state in Insights modal for inactive affiliates
functions.verdict: Comprehensive analytics module. The Insights modal with hourly activity is a differentiator. Main weakness: no automated reporting, no email digests, no alerting on performance thresholds. The 'Get Data' button pattern (manual refresh) adds friction compared to auto-refresh dashboards.

functions.purpose: Track First Time Deposit conversion events, reconcile affiliate payouts, and allow manual FTD corrections when brand CRM data is delayed or incorrect.
data_flow: Step 1: Brand CRM reports an FTD event (via API callback or manual upload via FTD Update)
Step 2: System matches FTD to the originating lead record and affiliate
Step 3: FTD appears in Falcon table with Approved or Declined status; Payout and Revenue columns populated
user_steps: Navigate to Falcon
Apply date range filter
Review FTD records table — identify Approved vs Declined conversions
Use 'FTD Update' (red) to manually add or correct FTD records
functions.automation_level: semi-automatic — FTDs received via brand API callback automatically; manual update option available
functions.ui_complexity: simple — table is dense but columns are self-explanatory
edge_cases_handled: Manual FTD Update flow for corrections
functions.verdict: Well-scoped module for FTD management. The 'FTD Issues' button (red) link suggests a dedicated workflow for investigating FTD discrepancies. The CPA action type in the Actions column indicates cost-per-acquisition model support.

functions.purpose: Dynamically substitute tracking parameters (UTM tags, affiliate sub-IDs, custom fields) when leads are forwarded to brand CRMs, enabling clean parameter mapping without changing the affiliate's original link.
data_flow: Step 1: User creates a Replace Params rule specifying Affiliate, Country, Campaign scope and the parameter substitutions
Step 2: When a lead matching the rule criteria is distributed, the system replaces the specified parameters before the API push
Step 3: Replaced params optionally written back to the lead record if 'Save in Leads' is ON
user_steps: Navigate to Replace Params
Click 'Create New Params'
Define rule name, affiliate scope, country, campaign, and parameter mappings
Toggle Status ON to activate
configuration_options: Affiliate scope (specific affiliate or All Affiliates)
Campaign scope (specific campaign or All Campaigns)
Replace Params Rule Priority in System Settings (First Rule wins)
functions.ui_complexity: simple — minimal UI; actual parameter mapping fields were not shown in frames
edge_cases_handled: Priority rule in System Settings handles conflicts between overlapping rules
functions.verdict: Niche but valuable feature for performance marketers managing multiple brand integrations with different parameter schemas. The 'Save in Leads' option adds flexibility. The priority system prevents ambiguity. Main gap: the actual parameter mapping UI (what gets replaced with what) was not visible in the recording.

cross_function_insights: Leads are the central data entity — everything else (Distribution, Falcon, Statistics, Replace Params) operates on or derives from lead records
The Lead Detail modal's API Request/Response log bridges the Leads and Distribution modules, showing what was sent to brands and what came back
Fraud Detection feeds into the Leads module (Fraud Score column) and can inform Distribution decisions (block low-quality leads from being routed)
The Falcon (FTD) module closes the loop from lead to deposit, enabling financial reconciliation with affiliates
Statistics aggregates across all lead/FTD data and presents it per-affiliate, creating the reporting layer on top of the operational data
Replace Params operates as a middleware layer between lead storage and brand push, modifying data in transit
notable_ux_patterns: SMOOTH: Multiple saved view tabs on Leads page — allows different team roles to have their own column presets without conflicting
SMOOTH: Skeleton loading states throughout — consistent loading UX that never shows broken/empty states
SMOOTH: Lead Detail modal with tabbed sections — keeps all lead data accessible without requiring page navigation
SMOOTH: Quick-select date range tabs (Today, Yesterday, Last 7 Days, etc.) — reduces clicks for the most common time ranges
FRICTION: Manual 'Get Data' button required after every date or filter change — adds cognitive overhead vs. automatic refresh
FRICTION: Icon-only sidebar by default — new users must learn icon meanings; no persistent text labels visible
FRICTION: Statistics Customize Columns modal uses a two-panel design but requires scrolling to find fields — a search box helps but fields are not grouped semantically
FRICTION: Dashboard date filter and Statistics/Leads date filters appear to be independent — changing the Dashboard filter does not carry over to other sections

feature_completeness.notes: Covers the full affiliate marketing operations stack: lead capture, routing, fraud detection, FTD tracking, parameter management, analytics, security, and employee management. Missing or not shown: automated alerts/notifications, scheduled reporting, A/B lead splitting, API documentation for affiliates, affiliate self-service portal, and mobile support.

ux_quality.notes: Dark mode enterprise aesthetic is well-executed and consistent. Skeleton loading states are polished. However, the 'Get Data' manual refresh pattern, icon-only navigation, and data-dense tables with minimal progressive disclosure create meaningful friction for non-expert users. The multiple saved view tabs are a standout UX decision.

tech_sophistication.notes: Real-time API integration logging at the lead level is technically sophisticated. Credit-based fraud API, bot detection, country/IP mismatch validation, and parameter replacement engine demonstrate mature backend logic. Scalability signals: 2,485 campaigns, 80 distribution rules, 100,000 daily lead cap. Missing evidence of: GraphQL, webhooks, advanced caching, or real-time push technology.

demo_effectiveness.notes: No audio makes it impossible to assess verbal pitch quality. Visually, the demo spends excessive time on the Leads list table (browsing without apparent purpose for large portions), which would likely lose audience attention. The Security section is shown briefly but is a compelling differentiator. Key modules like Affiliates, Offers, Boxes, Balance, Traffic Rules, and Alerts are shown only as sidebar icons and never opened.

innovation.notes: The combination of real-time API push logging (viewable per lead), credit-based fraud scoring, bot detection in the lead table, and parameter replacement on push is a reasonably differentiated feature set for the affiliate CRM category. The 'Insights' per-affiliate modal with hourly activity analysis adds depth beyond basic tabular analytics. Not breakthrough innovation but solid domain-specific execution.

scorecard.summary: trackbox.ai is a mature, domain-specific affiliate marketing CRM with a comprehensive feature set covering the full lead-to-FTD lifecycle. The platform is clearly built by practitioners who understand the operational needs of performance marketing networks — the API push logging, fraud scoring, FTD reconciliation, and parameter replacement engine are exactly what this market needs. The dark-mode enterprise UI is polished and consistent. The main weaknesses are UX friction (manual data refresh, icon-only navigation) and demo structure (too much time on lead list browsing, key modules never opened). The product appears well-suited to mid-to-large performance marketing networks running multi-brand, multi-affiliate CPA operations, particularly in financial verticals.
key_strengths: Full-stack affiliate operations: one platform covers traffic → leads → distribution → FTDs → payouts → analytics
API push transparency: every lead shows the exact JSON sent to brand CRM and the response received
Fraud and bot detection built into the lead management workflow
Parameter replacement engine for flexible brand integration without changing affiliate tracking links
Per-affiliate Insights modal with hourly activity drill-down
Security module with 2FA compliance tracking — rare in this category
White-label ready: custom system title, logo, and theme color
Scalable: 2,485+ campaigns, 100,000 daily lead cap, 80+ distribution rules in demo
key_weaknesses: Manual 'Get Data' refresh pattern throughout the platform creates unnecessary friction
10-rule limit on Distribution rules may constrain large networks
No visible automated alerts, threshold notifications, or scheduled email reports
Icon-only sidebar navigation is not self-explanatory for new users
Several modules visible in the sidebar (Affiliates, Offers, Boxes, Balance, Traffic Rules, Alerts, Payment Plans) were never demonstrated
Statistics Insights shows 'No Data' for many affiliates in demo — suggests data coverage gaps
No apparent affiliate self-service portal visible in the demo
features_worth_studying: Lead Detail modal with API Request/Response log tabs — gives support and ops teams full visibility into brand push failures without needing developer access
Multiple saved view tabs on the Leads page — simple but powerful way to support different role-based workflows in a shared data table
Per-lead Bot detection column with colored indicators — visual fraud signal at the list level before opening individual records
Insights modal with Hourly Activity tab — understanding which hours drive quality leads is actionable for affiliates and managers
Replace Params Rule Priority system in System Settings — explicit conflict resolution for overlapping rules is often overlooked in rule engine design
Security module with 2FA compliance counter (30 employees without 2FA) — proactive compliance tracking in a B2B SaaS is a trust differentiator
Distribution Daily/Hourly/Rules Limit counters at top of page — surfaces capacity constraints before users hit them during rule creation
FTD Update manual flow in Falcon — acknowledges that brand CRM webhooks fail and provides a graceful fallback without requiring developer intervention
